tuple-records-and-unpacking.label: Tuple Records and Unpacking
tuple-records-and-unpacking.summary: Use tuples when a small record should keep a fixed shape, then unpack each position into a meaningful name.

sketch0.title: A fixed record has a clear shape

try-tuple-records-sketch0.title: Create a location tuple
try-tuple-records-sketch0.prompt: In `main.py`, create `location` as the tuple `("Austin", "TX")`. Print the city from index 0 and the state from index 1 on separate lines.
try-tuple-records-sketch0.hint: Use parentheses for the fixed pair, then read each position with square brackets.

help.concept: A tuple stores an ordered record whose positions should not be changed after creation.

try-tuple-records-sketch0.starterCode: # Create a fixed location record and print both fields.

0.message: Create location as the required two-value tuple.

1.message: Read the first tuple position with location[0].

try-tuple-unpacking-sketch1.title: Unpack an event tuple
try-tuple-unpacking-sketch1.prompt: The starter defines `event = ("Workshop", 24)`. Unpack it into `title` and `seats`, then print the two variables on separate lines.
try-tuple-unpacking-sketch1.hint: Place both new names on the left side of one assignment.

help.concept: Tuple unpacking gives a meaningful name to each position in a fixed record.
help.hint_1: The number of names must match the number of tuple values.
help.hint_2: Print the unpacked variables, not hard-coded literals.

try-tuple-unpacking-sketch1.starterCode:
event = ("Workshop", 24)
# Unpack event into title and seats, then print both values.
try-tuple-unpacking-sketch1.solutionCode:
event = ("Workshop", 24)
title, seats = event
print(title)
print(seats)

0.message: Unpack event into title and seats in one assignment.

sc-tuple-purpose.title: Choose the fixed record
sc-tuple-purpose.prompt: Which value is the best tuple when a coordinate should keep exactly two ordered positions?
sc-tuple-purpose.hint: Look for the ordered collection whose structure is intended to stay fixed.

help.concept: Tuples are useful for compact ordered records that should not be mutated.
help.hint_1: Parentheses distinguish this literal from a list.
help.hint_2: A dictionary names fields rather than relying on positions.

mc-tuple-facts.title: Recognize tuple behavior
mc-tuple-facts.prompt: Which statements about tuples are true?

options.b: append() adds a value to an existing tuple.
options.c: A tuple can be unpacked into multiple names.

mc-tuple-facts.hint: Separate how a tuple is read from whether its structure can be mutated.

help.concept: Tuples are ordered and indexable, but they do not support list-style mutation methods.
help.hint_2: A tuple may hold values of different types.

fb-tuple-index.title: Read the first tuple field
fb-tuple-index.prompt: Complete the expression that reads `"North"` from `direction = ("North", 12)`: `direction[___]`
fb-tuple-index.hint: Tuple indexing begins at the same position as list and string indexing.

help.concept: Every Python sequence begins with the zero index.
help.hint_1: The requested value is the first field.

dr-tuple-unpack.title: Build an unpacking assignment
dr-tuple-unpack.prompt: Arrange the tokens to unpack `record` into `name` and `score`.
dr-tuple-unpack.hint: Put the target names on the left and the tuple variable on the right.

help.concept: Unpacking is one assignment with one target name per tuple position.
help.hint_1: The comma separates the two target names.
help.hint_2: The tuple variable appears after the equals sign.

try_tuple_records_sketch0.title: Try it yourself
try_tuple_records_sketch0.prompt: Create and read a location tuple.

try_tuple_unpacking_sketch1.title: Try it yourself
try_tuple_unpacking_sketch1.prompt: Unpack an event tuple into two names.

sketch-1.title: A fixed record has a clear shape
sketch-1.bodyMarkdown:
A list is useful when a program expects items to grow, shrink, or change. A tuple communicates a different intention: these values form one ordered record whose shape should remain stable.

```python
rgb = (240, 128, 64)
print(rgb[0])
```

The positions still use zero-based indexing, but methods such as `append()` are not available. That limitation is useful when the record represents something fixed, such as a coordinate, color, or date.

sketch-2.title: Unpack positions into names
sketch-2.bodyMarkdown:
Indexes are compact, but named variables make the meaning of each field easier to read. Tuple unpacking assigns every position in one step.

```python
book = ("Kindred", 1979)
title, year = book
print(f"{title} was published in {year}.")
```

The number of target names must match the number of tuple fields. Once unpacked, the rest of the program can work with `title` and `year` instead of remembering what positions 0 and 1 mean.
